reason: The statement says 'phi cannot be an extreme point of any set C defined by finitely many affine constraints' but does not specify that these affine constraints must be CONTINUOUS (or at least lower-semicontinuous) on the state space. This continuity requirement is essential -- it is a hypothesis of the Weis-Shirokov theorem (Node 1.2) which underlies Node 1.3. Without specifying this, the statement could be misread as applying to discontinuous affine functionals, for which the result does not hold. FIX: Add the qualifier 'continuous affine constraints' or reference the hypotheses of Node 1.3 explicitly.
